Title: A large conserved family of small-molecule carboxyl methyltransferases identified from microorganisms
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports methyl ester formation by relieving SAH inhibition
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Added to methyltransferase reactions to hydrolyze inhibitory SAH
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Sequentially acts with LuxS in SAH degradation system
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances methyl ester production indirectly by removing the feedback inhibitor SAH
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful coupling enzyme for metabolic engineering and biocatalytic methylation systems to improve conversion
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Requires coupling with LuxS for full SAH turnover
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SahE was used to degrade SAH, a potent inhibitor of OPCMT_like enzymes. 2. Adding SahE together with LuxS increased methylation conversion to >96%. 3. It acts as an enabling auxiliary factor rather than the primary methyltransferase.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports methyl ester formation by completing SAH detoxification pathway
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Added to methyltransferase reactions with SahE
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Converts S-ribosylhomocysteine to homocysteine after SahE action
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves methyl ester production indirectly by relieving SAH-mediated feedback inhibition
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory enzyme in engineered methylation cascades
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LuxS was coupled with SahE to remove SAH inhibition in OPCMT_like reactions. 2. Its addition improved product conversion markedly. 3. It is an accessory homeostasis/engineering factor rather than the focal catalytic gene.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Steroid acid methyl esterification / small-molecule carboxylate methylation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 3-oxo-4-pregnene-20-carboxyl (3-OPC); also accepts cholic acid, 3-indoleacrylic acid, indole-3-acetic acid, quinaldic acid, 2-naphthoic acid, cinnamic acid derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from S-adenosyl-L-methionine to the carboxyl group of 3-OPC to form 3-OPC methyl ester
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: OPCMT_like methyltransferase; leucine carboxyl methyltransferase (LCM) fold; class I SAM-dependent methyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Proposed role in microbial carboxylic acid/steroid homeostasis; may reduce toxicity of sterol acids
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays with SAM; SAH strongly inhibits activity; SAH nucleosidase and LuxS relieve inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step; major OPCMT in M. neoaurum based on highest catalytic efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes highly efficient formation of 3-OPC methyl ester and broad carboxylate methyl ester production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant overexpression and purification; in vitro enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; gene knockout series; metabolite profiling; ligand-binding assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising biocatalyst for industrial methyl ester production due to broad substrate scope and high activity; coupling with SAH degradation or SAM regeneration is recommended
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: At least one OPCMT_like gene appears required for survival/homeostasis, so full elimination may impair viability; SAH feedback inhibition reduces activity unless relieved
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MnMT4 was identified as the SAM-dependent enzyme directly converting 3-OPC to 3-OPC methyl ester. 2. MnMT4 showed the highest catalytic efficiency among five M. neoaurum OPCMT_like proteins (kcat 765.3 min^-1; kcat/Km 1.56 µM^-1 min^-1), indicating a major role in 3-OPC methyl ester synthesis. 3. MnMT4 also methylated diverse carboxylic acids, supporting a role in broad acid homeostasis and biocatalytic applications.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inactive toward 3-OPDC was not explicitly reported here for MnMT4 in purified assay; initial family identification distinguished it from SABATH CbMTs; SAH binds much more tightly than SAM

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Steroid acid methyl esterification / small-molecule carboxylate methylation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 3-OPC; also cholic acid, 3-indoleacrylic acid, indole-3-acetic acid, 2-naphthoic acid, quinaldic acid, cinnamic acid derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 3-OPC methyl ester and other methyl esters
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: SAM-dependent methylation of carboxyl groups on 3-OPC and other rigid carboxylic acids
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: OPCMT_like methyltransferase; LCM fold; class I SAM-dependent methyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays with SAM; SAH nucleosidase and LuxS added in many assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to 3-OPC methyl ester formation and broad carboxylate methylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; sequential gene knockout
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful auxiliary enzyme for methyl ester biocatalysis with broad substrate scope
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MnMT2 is homologous to MnMT4 and catalyzes 3-OPC methylation. 2. It shows substantial but lower catalytic efficiency than MnMT4. 3. It contributes redundantly to carboxylate methylation in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of the five-gene OPCMT_like cluster/function set

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Steroid acid methyl esterification / small-molecule carboxylate methylation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 3-OPC; limited activity on cholic acid and a few aromatic acids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: SAM-dependent carboxyl methylation of 3-OPC with relatively narrow substrate scope and low efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: OPCMT_like methyltransferase; LCM fold; class I SAM-dependent methyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides residual methylation activity contributing to tolerance/homeostasis when other mnmt genes are deleted
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; gene knockout in Δ3mnmt background
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less preferred catalyst due to narrow scope/low efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MnMT3 catalyzes 3-OPC methylation but with the lowest efficiency among tested active enzymes. 2. It still contributes to residual methylation capacity in vivo. 3. Removal of mnmt3 in the Δ3mnmt background further lowered acid tolerance, supporting redundancy.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Substrate recognition more specific than MnMT2/4/6

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Steroid acid methyl esterification / small-molecule carboxylate methylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: SAM-dependent methylation of 3-OPC carboxyl group with moderate to low efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: OPCMT_like methyltransferase; LCM fold; class I SAM-dependent methyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides remaining methylation activity sufficient to maintain viability when other four mnmt genes are disrupted
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; gene deletion attempts
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential backup biocatalyst; biologically important for sustaining methylation capacity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could be conditionally essential in multi-knockout background; final deletion from Δ4mnmt unsuccessful
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MnMT5 methylates 3-OPC with lower efficiency than MnMT4/MnMT2. 2. It retains enough activity to sustain carboxyl methylation in the Δ4mnmt mutant background. 3. Failure to delete mnmt5 from Δ4mnmt suggests at least one OPCMT_like enzyme is required for survival/homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Can be deleted in wild type but not after deletion of the other four OPCMT_like genes

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Steroid acid methyl esterification / small-molecule carboxylate methylation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 3-OPC; also cholic acid, 3-indoleacrylic acid, indole-3-acetic acid, 2-naphthoic acid, quinaldic acid, cinnamic acid derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 3-OPC methyl ester and other methyl esters
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: SAM-dependent methylation of carboxyl groups on 3-OPC and multiple rigid aromatic/steroidal acids
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: OPCMT_like methyltransferase; LCM fold; class I SAM-dependent methyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to broad-spectrum carboxylate methylation and 3-OPC methyl ester formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; gene knockout
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential broad-substrate catalyst, though weaker than MnMT4
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower efficiency than MnMT4 and MnMT2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MnMT6 catalyzes methylation of 3-OPC and several additional rigid carboxylic acids. 2. Its substrate scope is broad but catalytic efficiency is clearly below MnMT4. 3. It contributes redundantly to in vivo methylation/homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ranked third in substrate range and catalytic efficiency among the five enzymes

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Steroid acid methyl esterification / small-molecule carboxylate methylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: SAM-dependent methylation of 3-OPC to 3-OPC methyl ester
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports steroid acid homeostasis in Mycobacteria
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Efficiently converts 3-OPC to its methyl ester in M. smegmatis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cell lysate assay; recombinant protein assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative microbial biocatalyst for methyl ester synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsMT1 is the closest M. smegmatis homolog of MnMT4. 2. It efficiently catalyzes 3-OPC methylation, reaching complete conversion after 12 h. 3. This supports widespread distribution of OPCMT_like genes in mycobacteria.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Steroid acid methyl esterification / small-molecule carboxylate methylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: SAM-dependent methylation of 3-OPC carboxyl group; structural prototype of OPCMT_like family
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: OPCMT_like methyltransferase; class I SAM-dependent methyltransferase with unique N-terminal helical domain and LCM-like fold
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Docking/MD with SAM and 3-OPC; mutagenesis of Thr20/Tyr194/W229
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes 3-OPC methylation; defines catalytic residues and structure of the new OPCMT_like family
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant expression; enzyme assay; structural analysis; mutagenesis; molecular docking and dynamics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Structural template for enzyme engineering and inhibitor design targeting pathogen homologs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ML2640c from M. leprae was experimentally verified as an OPCMT_like carboxyl methyltransferase. 2. Structural and mutational analyses showed Thr20 and Tyr194 are crucial for orienting the carboxyl substrate for methyl transfer. 3. Its large hydrophobic pocket explains broad substrate acceptance and provides a basis for engineering or inhibitor design.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PDB 2UYQ; monomeric enzyme; T20V/Y194F double mutant abolished activity

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Steroid acid methyl esterification / small-molecule carboxylate methylation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: OPCMT_like methyltransferase; class I SAM-dependent methyltransferase with unique N-terminal helical domain
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Proposed role in acid/steroid homeostasis in pathogenic mycobacteria
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Verified as an active OPCMT_like enzyme capable of complete conversion of 3-OPC to its methyl ester
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant expression and biochemical assay; structural comparison
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antimicrobial target and scaffold for inhibitor development in mycobacterial pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rv0731c from M. tuberculosis was biochemically validated as an OPCMT_like CbMT. 2. It completely converted 3-OPC to 3-OPC methyl ester. 3. Its conservation in pathogens supports family-wide physiological relevance and targetability.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PDB 6ID6; monomeric and structurally overlaps with ML2640c
